subject: Michael Sonnenfeldt
body:
-----Original Message-----
From: Michael Sonnenfeldt [mailto:michaelw@sonnenfeldt.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, June 10, 2008 11:14 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Fwd: Delivery Status Notification (Failure)

>
>
>
> Hi Lesley
> We manufacture Solar Powered lights, so we have a number of  
> different solar powered light systems that together could light an  
> entire island's paths, roadways, driveways, signs, patios etc.  L:et  
> me know what would be useful and send Jeffrey my regards.  Best-  
> Michael
> On Jun 10, 2008, at 10:15 AM, Lesley Groff wrote:
>
>> Hello Michael...Jeffrey has reviewed your email and would like to  
>> know
>> if you would be interested in providing solar power to his island.
>> Could you please let me know?
>>
>> Thanks so much,
>>
>> Lesley Groff
>> Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
>>
>> -----Original Message-----
>> From: Michael Sonnenfeldt [mailto:michaelw@sonnenfeldt.com]
>> Sent: Monday, June 09, 2008 2:27 PM
>> To: Lesley Groff
>> Subject: Some important news about SOL Inc I am pleased to share with
>> you
>>
>>
>>
>> Dear Jeffrey:
>>
>> I am excited to share with you the attached press release from Sol  
>> Inc.,
>> the leading manufacturer of commercial and industrial outdoor solar
>> powered lighting systems, which I am proud to Chair.  The press  
>> release
>> I am enclosing is about a recent installation of solar powered  
>> lighting
>> systems in 11 villages in Peru that were devastated by last summer's
>> earthquake.
>>
>> I wanted to share the announcement with you for a number of reasons
>> beyond the obvious personal pride for Sol's ability to help bring  
>> light
>> and hope to these devastated areas.
>>
>> The announcement displays our new look and tag line:
>> "Reliable Renewable Remarkable"
>> which reflects our commitment to quality, the environment, and the
>> communities we serve.
>>
>> In the last year, Sol's growth as the leading manufacturer of solar
>> powered outdoor lighting systems has accelerated and we expect to
>> deliver over 7,000 systems around the world during 2008 with
>> significantly greater deliveries expected in 2009.  We recently
>> activated a global supply chain to ensure we remain competitive with
>> competitors around the globe.
>>
>> Our new solar powered LED lights are super bright and efficient.  
>> Our new
>> street light is currently being tested to evaluate the energy savings
>> for replacing the luminaire in retrofit projects across the country.
>>
>> Sol is the largest outdoor solar lighting supplier to U.S. Military
>> bases around the world and we already have solar powered lighting
>> systems installed in over 50 countries on six continents. In  
>> Africa, the
>> capital city of Conakrie in Guinea is rapidly becoming the first
>> national capital on the planet to have a majority of its streets  
>> lit by
>> solar powered streetlights - all Sol products, to date.
>>
>> In Boston, we provide solar powered advertising panels for local bus
>> shelters, and our latest solar powered roadside billboard system is
>> being installed in California.
>>
>> I look forward to sharing more announcements of installations that  
>> can
>> help change the world by reducing our dependence on fossil fuels  
>> while
>> helping to save the environment - one solar light at a time.
>>
>> In the meantime, if you have ideas that can help us improve our  
>> products
>> or better serve our customers, please feel free to call or write.
>>
>> Look forward to hearing from you.
>>
>> All my very best.
>>
>> Michael
>>
>>
>
>
>

body_html:
-----Original Message-----
From: Michael Sonnenfeldt [mailto:michaelw@sonnenfeldt.com]
Sent: Tuesday, June 10, 2008 11:14 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Fwd: Delivery Status Notification (Failure)

Hi Lesley
We manufacture Solar Powered lights, so we have a number of
different solar powered light systems that together could light an
entire island's paths, roadways, driveways, signs, patios etc. L:et
me know what would be useful and send Jeffrey my regards. Best-
Michael
On Jun 10, 2008, at 10:15 AM, Lesley Groff wrote:

Hello Michael...Jeffrey has reviewed your email and would like to
know
if you would be interested in providing solar power to his island.
Could you please let me know?

Thanks so much,

Lesley Groff
Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein

-----Original Message-----
From: Michael Sonnenfeldt [mailto:michaelw@sonnenfeldt.com]
Sent: Monday, June 09, 2008 2:27 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Some important news about SOL Inc I am pleased to share with
you

Dear Jeffrey:

I am excited to share with you the attached press release from Sol
Inc.,
the leading manufacturer of commercial and industrial outdoor solar
powered lighting systems, which I am proud to Chair. The press
release
I am enclosing is about a recent installation of solar powered
lighting
systems in 11 villages in Peru that were devastated by last summer's
earthquake.

I wanted to share the announcement with you for a number of reasons
beyond the obvious personal pride for Sol's ability to help bring
light
and hope to these devastated areas.

The announcement displays our new look and tag line:
"Reliable Renewable Remarkable"
which reflects our commitment to quality, the environment, and the
communities we serve.

In the last year, Sol's growth as the leading manufacturer of solar
powered outdoor lighting systems has accelerated and we expect to
deliver over 7,000 systems around the world during 2008 with
significantly greater deliveries expected in 2009. We recently
activated a global supply chain to ensure we remain competitive with
competitors around the globe.

Our new solar powered LED lights are super bright and efficient.
Our new
street light is currently being tested to evaluate the energy savings
for replacing the luminaire in retrofit projects across the country.

Sol is the largest outdoor solar lighting supplier to U.S. Military
bases around the world and we already have solar powered lighting
systems installed in over 50 countries on six continents. In
Africa, the
capital city of Conakrie in Guinea is rapidly becoming the first
national capital on the planet to have a majority of its streets
lit by
solar powered streetlights - all Sol products, to date.

In Boston, we provide solar powered advertising panels for local bus
shelters, and our latest solar powered roadside billboard system is
being installed in California.

I look forward to sharing more announcements of installations that
can
help change the world by reducing our dependence on fossil fuels
while
helping to save the environment - one solar light at a time.

In the meantime, if you have ideas that can help us improve our
products
or better serve our customers, please feel free to call or write.

Look forward to hearing from you.

All my very best.

Michael
